subsections.text: The commission shall establish a compliance program, which must include a compliance office to oversee the program. The compliance office is responsible for:
acting to prevent and detect serious breaches of departmental policy, fraud, waste, and abuse of office, including any acts of criminal conduct within the department;
independently and objectively reviewing, investigating, delegating, and overseeing the investigation of:

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: allegations of wrongdoing by department employees;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: crimes committed on department property; and
overseeing the operation of the telephone hotline established under Section 201.211 (Ethics Affirmation and Hotline) ;
ensuring that members of the commission and department employees receive appropriate ethics training; and
performing other duties assigned to the office by the commission.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1345 (S.B.1420), Sec. 15(a), eff. September 1, 2011.
